_note: Unified taxonomy: tier1/2/3 topic hierarchy + tier4 content-gen seeds + gallery tag → topic mapping. Consolidates the previous topic_tag_mappings.json + categorized_tags.json + subject_topic_seeds.json + the EXTRA_TAG_TO_TOPICS block in topicRegistry.ts. Single source of truth — lib/topicRegistry.ts, lib/searchIndex.ts, lib/relatedTags.ts, scripts/lib/auto_tag.cjs, scripts/generate_template_examples.cjs all read from here.

tier4._note: Leaf topic names grouped by their tier-3 subject. Input to scripts/generate_template_examples.cjs for content drops. Add new subjects here; existing code (auto-tag pipeline, search index) keys off the subject name in tier3.language / tier3.learning.

_gallery_tag_to_topics_note: Gallery-prompt tag → tier-2/tier-3 topic(s) it maps to. Used by (1) lib/topicRegistry.ts getTopicsForTag — tag-page surfaces templates from these topics; (2) lib/relatedTags.ts getRelatedTags — tags sharing a topic are siblings. Replaces the previous flat-list categorized_tags.json categories with topic-aligned destinations.
_reverse_map_excluded_topics_note: Topics excluded from the reverse map only — their topic page still pulls a gallery row via TOPIC_GALLERY_TAG, but the tag page does NOT surface their templates. Use when a topic's template set is too heterogeneous to make a coherent Templates section.
_content_shapes_note: shape_id -> { label, description, templates: [...] }. The 'shape' is the format-pattern (1v1 battle, grid, map, etc.) portable across subjects. Hand-curated by scripts/build_template_shapes.cjs as of 2026-05-30. Re-derive after any nano_templates.json change or shape catalog update.

vs-1v1-battle.label: 1v1 / VS comparison
vs-1v1-battle.description: Head-to-head matchup between two distinct subjects (sports rivalries, character battles, MBTI vs MBTI, word-vs-word).

then-vs-now.label: Then vs now / before-after
then-vs-now.description: Temporal comparison of the same subject across two states (generation gap, decade-vs-decade, stereotype-vs-reality, before-after transformation).

grid-collection.label: Grid / collection
grid-collection.description: N-cell visual grid surfacing many items at once (top-10, MBTI 16-cell, fandom grids, variety posters, photo grids, collection illustrations).

team-group-poster.label: Team / group poster
team-group-poster.description: Ensemble portrait of multiple subjects on a single composition (World Cup team, sitcom cast, MBTI group, vocab-category group).

timeline-evolution.label: Timeline / evolution
timeline-evolution.description: Chronological progression along a path or timeline (history-timeline, evolution arc, life-journey, clothing evolution).

nostalgia-retro.label: Nostalgia / retro aesthetic
nostalgia-retro.description: Vintage, retro, or nostalgic styling regardless of subject (first-item poster, retro infographic, vintage stamp, old-money style).

map-spatial.label: Map / spatial layout
map-spatial.description: Geographic or spatial layout — landmarks, regional themes, travel maps, historical event maps, word-origins maps.

recipe-card.label: Recipe card
recipe-card.description: Food recipe with ingredients + steps in a card format.

how-to-guide.label: How-to / step-by-step guide
how-to-guide.description: Instructional guide with explicit steps or annotated diagram (beauty step-by-step, gardening how-to, sports technique, watercolor tutorial).

educational-explainer.label: Educational / explainer infographic
educational-explainer.description: Single-topic explainer that teaches what-is or how-works (science facts, weather education, weird knowledge, financial habit, lifestyle infographic, self-help summary).

mbti-portrait.label: MBTI personality portrait
mbti-portrait.description: MBTI 16-personality framework applied to a fandom or domain (single MBTI, group MBTI, MBTI in love, character-as-MBTI).

vocabulary-flashcard.label: Vocabulary / language flashcard
vocabulary-flashcard.description: Word + image learning card across any language (English/Chinese/multilingual vocab, idiom card, phrasal verbs, kids vocab, phonics).

character-profile.label: Character / figure profile
character-profile.description: Single-subject character or historical-figure card with traits, biography, or analysis.

collage-scrapbook.label: Collage / scrapbook
collage-scrapbook.description: Mixed-media composition (travel journal collage, ethnic-costume deconstruction board, watercolor theme collage).

lookbook-outfit.label: Lookbook / outfit / styling
lookbook-outfit.description: Fashion outfit, hairstyle, or makeover styling display (try-on, gown design sheet, nail art, ethnic costume, portrait retouching).

packaging-design.label: Packaging / product design
packaging-design.description: Product packaging, promotional poster, industrial-design sketch, mood board, ecommerce-style product visual.

celebration-festival.label: Celebration / festival poster
celebration-festival.description: Event- or holiday-themed visual (cultural festival, lunar new year red envelope, self-care illustration).

watercolor-illustration.label: Watercolor / ink-wash aesthetic
watercolor-illustration.description: Watercolor, ink-wash, or guofeng stylistic shape — applied across subjects (watercolor map, ink-wash poetry, watercolor vocab).

quote-typography-poster.label: Quote / typography poster
quote-typography-poster.description: Typography-driven single-message poster (quote poster, old-money name styling).

hero-thematic-poster.label: Hero / thematic poster
hero-thematic-poster.description: Single artistic poster anchored on a theme, IP, or wallpaper (movie poster, media-theme wallpaper, theme-overview, book series).

_template_shapes_note: template_id -> array of shape_ids this template implements. Reverse of content_shapes. Consumed by: content-gap discovery (shape × subject matrix), per-shape landing pages, Reddit demand router, search-generation bridge Phase 3 accept conditions.
_information_types_note: Information Type vocabulary — how content is *organized* (Fact/Profile/Collection/Comparison/Timeline/Process/InfoCard/Vocabulary/Dialogue/Quote/Map/Quiz/Story/Insight). 13 canonical types. Sister axis to subject (tier1-4) and style (content_styles). Auto-derived by scripts/build_template_information_types.cjs scanning nano_templates.json topics[] against each type's topic_word. Several types reuse existing topic words by design (profile=portrait, collection=groups, process=guides, timeline=history; comparison/vocabulary/dialogue direct-collide with same-named tier-2 subjects — same word, both senses). Per-template source of truth is nano_templates.json topics[]; this map is a derived index.

insight.label: Insight (fact + analysis)
insight.description: Cold-knowledge claims and structured deep-dives — one-line trivia, book summaries, hot-event breakdowns, framework explainers.

profile.label: Profile / single-subject
profile.description: One subject's attributes — name + image + key traits. Reuses the existing tier2.character.portrait topic name.

collection.label: Collection / N-items
collection.description: Multiple instances together (9 fruits, 16 birds, top-10 destinations). Reuses tier2.character.groups.

comparison.label: Comparison / A vs B
comparison.description: Side-by-side contrast. Direct collision with tier2.character.comparison — same word, both senses.

timeline.label: Timeline / chronology
timeline.description: Events along time (history-timelines, evolution arcs, life-journeys). Reuses tier2.learning.history.

process.label: Process / step-by-step
process.description: Ordered how-to (recipes, beauty step-by-step, gardening). Reuses tier2.lifestyle.guides.

information-card.label: Information card / encyclopedia entry
information-card.description: Attribute-rich catalog entry (herb, plant, species, landmark, intangible heritage).

vocabulary.label: Vocabulary / word + image
vocabulary.description: Word + image learning unit. Direct collision with tier2.language.vocabulary — same word, both senses.

dialogue.label: Dialogue / conversation
dialogue.description: Conversation bubbles, native expressions, sentence-pair drills.

quote.label: Quote / one-message poster
quote.description: Name + quote + (optional) portrait. Single-message typography-led poster.

map.label: Map / spatial layout
map.description: Geographic or spatial layout — landmark map, travel map, historical-event map, word-origins map.

quiz.label: Quiz / personality test / matching chart
quiz.description: Choice-driven self-test or fandom-mapping (MBTI, personality choice, matching chart, 9-traits).

story.label: Story / multi-panel narrative
story.description: Multi-panel narrative — travel-journal, scroll storytelling, life-journey-as-story. Distinct from Timeline.

_template_information_types_note: template_id -> sorted [type_id, ...]. Reverse of information_types. Auto-derived from nano_templates.json topics[].
_content_styles_note: Style axis (Round 2B, 2026-05-31). 4 sub-axes: mood, aesthetic, lighting, temporal. Each entry carries gallery_count from lib/generated/nanobanana_prompts_metadata.json — the demand signal from prompt-side usage. Sister to information_types (info-type axis) and template_subjects (subject axis). Per-template style tagging deferred — style is query-side vocabulary; templates that carry style do so via their own topics[] arrays.

mood.label: Mood / emotional tone
mood.description: How the image *feels* — the emotional / temperamental tone the prompt asks for, independent of subject or medium. Drives query intent like 'cozy autumn aesthetic' or 'edgy editorial portrait'.

aesthetic.label: Aesthetic / visual style
aesthetic.description: The visual medium and rendering style — what the image looks like as an artifact (photorealistic vs illustration vs ink-wash, monochrome vs vibrant, vintage vs y2k).

lighting.label: Lighting / illumination
lighting.description: How the scene is lit — distinct from temporal because lighting can be fully synthetic (neon, studio). Some entries overlap with temporal (golden hour reads as both lighting and time-of-day).

temporal.label: Temporal / seasonal / occasion
temporal.description: When the scene takes place — season, time of day, weather, holiday. Distinct from lighting because temporal anchors a real-world moment (christmas, winter, sunset).

_template_subjects_note: template_id → array of taxonomy subjects this template's `topics` array references. Inclusive match across tier1 ∪ tier2 ∪ tier3 entries. Auto-derived by scripts/build_template_subjects.cjs from public/data/nano_templates.json. Consumed by: future search→generation bridge (per docs/search-generation-bridge.md), auto-tag pipeline candidate-set narrowing, content-gap analyzer. Re-derive after every nano_templates.json change.
